summary: DHH highlights a Java developer's grudging admission that Ruby on Rails is impressively superior to Java for web development. Chris Nelson, recommended by Rake creator Jim Weirich, watched the Rails demo video at a Cincinnati Java Users Group meeting and was blown away despite his loyalty to Java. DHH playfully needles the Java community by suggesting the realization will only bring tears. The post captures the early Rails evangelism era when Java developers were first encountering Rails.
key_points: A Java users group member was recommended to check out Rails and found it devastatingly good compared to Java
The Rails demo video was a key evangelism tool that converted skeptics across language communities
DHH uses the Java developer's own words to let the contrast between Rails and Java speak for itself
Early Rails adoption was spreading through word-of-mouth within established Java communities
money_quotes: And gosh darn it, it's just too darn good. Makes me sick.
I'm no Ruby guy, believe me you. I don't go in for that alternative language kind of thing one bit. Unamerican if ya ask me. But confound it, I was blown away.
I'd advise a big box of Kleenex for work tomorrow, Chris.
key_insight: Rails was so compelling that even loyal Java developers couldn't deny its superiority after seeing the demo, marking the beginning of a wave of conversions from Java to Ruby.
